steps.title: Group factors by base
steps.content: Rewrite the product so like bases sit together: $(m^4 \cdot m^1)(q^4 \cdot q^5)(z^{-1} \cdot z^3)$.

steps.title: Add exponents for same bases
steps.content:
Using $a^m \cdot a^n = a^{m+n}$:
• $m$: $4 + 1 = 5$
• $q$: $4 + 5 = 9$
• $z$: $-1 + 3 = 2$
The product simplifies to $m^5 q^9 z^2$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• "${m}^{4}{q}^{20}{z}^{-3}$": Multiplies the $q$ exponents and subtracts $z$ exponents instead of adding — wrong.
  • "${m}^{5}{q}^{9}{z}^{2}$": Exponents $5$, $9$, $2$ match the sums.
  • "${m}^{6}{q}^{8}{z}^{-1}$": Exponents off by $1$ in each variable — wrong.
  • "${m}^{20}{q}^{12}{z}^{-2}$": Multiplies exponents instead of adding — wrong.
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
